prevalence.value: low endemic transmission; outbreaks occur in defined risk networks and via foodborne events
prevalence.trend: stable overall; periodic outbreaks (MSM networks, foodborne) and travel-related cases

burden.mortalityRate: Very low; risk increases with older age and chronic liver disease

riskFactors: low baseline immunity in many countries
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute hepatitis syndrome with anti-HAV IgM; outbreak linkage investigation per ECDC guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; case-based detection and outbreak investigation
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High recovery; control depends on vaccination strategies and rapid public health response

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Strong laboratory confirmation and genotyping used for outbreak tracing in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; vaccination and contact prophylaxis per national/ECDC guidance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Outbreak control in high-contact networks and food supply chain investigations
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust public health surveillance and outbreak response capacity across member states
